Emergency.recommendation: Please visit the emergency department immediately.

General Medicine / OPD.handles: Common illnesses, fever, cold, minor infections
General Medicine / OPD.recommendation: Please visit the General Medicine OPD during 8AM–6PM or schedule an appointment for non-urgent checkup.

Cardiology.recommendation: For new or worsening chest pain/palpitations, go to Emergency. For routine checkup or stable symptoms, schedule a Cardiology OPD visit between 9AM–5PM.

Orthopedics.recommendation: For suspected fractures or severe trauma, go to Emergency. For mild joint/back pain, visit Orthopedics OPD during 9AM–5PM for evaluation.

Dental.recommendation: For uncontrolled bleeding or facial swelling, go to Emergency. Otherwise, book an appointment with Dental OPD between 9AM–5PM.

Neurology.recommendation: Sudden numbness, confusion, or stroke symptoms – go to Emergency immediately. For chronic headaches or follow-up, visit Neurology OPD during 9AM–5PM.

Pediatrics.recommendation: For high fever with lethargy or breathing difficulty, go to Pediatric Emergency. For routine checkups or mild symptoms, visit Pediatrics OPD between 8AM–8PM.

Pulmonology.recommendation: Severe breathing difficulty or bluish lips – go to Emergency. For asthma follow-up or chronic cough, schedule a Pulmonology OPD visit (9AM–5PM).

Gastroenterology.recommendation: Severe abdominal pain, bloody vomit/stool – go to Emergency. For acidity or mild diarrhea, visit Gastroenterology OPD during 9AM–5PM.

Ophthalmology.recommendation: Sudden vision loss or chemical exposure – go to Emergency. For redness or mild pain, schedule an Ophthalmology OPD visit (9AM–5PM).

ENT.recommendation: Severe throat swelling or inability to swallow – go to Emergency. For ear pain or sinus issues, visit ENT OPD between 9AM–5PM.

Dermatology.recommendation: For rash with difficulty breathing or swollen lips/face – go to Emergency. For acne or eczema, schedule a Dermatology OPD visit (9AM–5PM).

Psychiatry.recommendation: If suicidal thoughts or immediate crisis – call crisis line (24/7) or go to Emergency. For anxiety/depression, schedule a Psychiatry OPD appointment (9AM–5PM).

Urology.recommendation: Severe flank pain with inability to urinate – go to Emergency. For blood in urine or mild UTI symptoms, visit Urology OPD during 9AM–5PM.

Gynecology.recommendation: Severe pelvic pain, heavy bleeding, or possible ectopic pregnancy – go to Emergency. For routine Pap smear or irregular periods, schedule a Gynecology OPD visit (9AM–5PM).

Endocrinology.recommendation: For severe hypoglycemia (unconsciousness) or thyroid storm (fever + rapid pulse) – go to Emergency. For diabetes or thyroid follow-up, visit Endocrinology OPD between 9AM–5PM.
